Investigators from RWTH Aachen University Have Reported New Data on Anxiety Disorders (Emetophobia - A Metacognitive Therapeutic Approach for an Overlooked Disorder) Date: Feb. 3, 2018 From: Psychology & Psychiatry Journal Publisher: NewsRX LLC Document Type: Brief article; Report Length: 353 words

Full Text: 2018 FEB 3 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Psychology & Psychiatry Journal -- Researchers detail new data in Mental Health Diseases and Conditions - Anxiety Disorders. According to news originating from Aachen, Germany, by NewsRx correspondents, research stated, "Emetophobia is the specific fear of vomiting that usually commences during childhood and adolescence. Cognitive behavioral therapy aims to expose patients to vomiting." Our news journalists obtained a quote from the research from RWTH Aachen University, "In this paper, a newly developed metacognitive concept and treatment approach to this disorder is illustrated within a small case series. Three adolescent girls with emetophobia were treated with metacognitive therapy (MCT). Measures of anxiety, worry, depression, and metacognitions before and after the treatment were documented. All patients recovered during the course of 8 to 11 sessions, and measurements of anxiety, worry, depression, and metacognitions dropped markedly." According to the news editors, the research concluded: "MCT presents a valuable treatment option for emetophobia in adolescents." For more information on this research see: Emetophobia - A Metacognitive Therapeutic Approach for an Overlooked Disorder. Zeitschrift Fur Kinder-Und Jugendpsychiatrie Und Psychotherapie, 2018;46(1):57-66. Zeitschrift Fur Kinder-Und Jugendpsychiatrie Und Psychotherapie can be contacted at: Verlag Hans Huber, Langgass-Strasse 76, Ch-3000 Bern 9, Switzerland. The news correspondents report that additional information may be obtained from M. Simons, Rhein Westfal TH Aachen, Dept. of Child & Adolescent Psychiat Psychosomat & Ps, Aachen, Germany. The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi-org.proxy.uba.uva.nl/10.1024/1422-4917/a000464.
